source_title: Geospatial Python - Full Course for Beginners with Geopandas

concepts.name: GeoPandas
concepts.definition: A Python library that extends the pandas library to allow spatial operations on geometric data types.

concepts.name: Pandas
concepts.definition: A foundational Python library for working with tabular data structures (DataFrames) and performing data manipulation/analysis.

concepts.name: GeoDataFrame
concepts.definition: A tabular data structure that extends the pandas DataFrame to include a geometry column and spatial attributes (CRS).

concepts.name: GeoSeries
concepts.definition: A column in a GeoDataFrame that specifically stores geometric objects (e.g., Points, Polygons) and spatial metadata.

concepts.name: CRS (Coordinate Reference System)
concepts.definition: A coordinate-based local, regional or global system used to locate geographical entities, stored as metadata within the geometry column.

concepts.name: Jupyter Notebook
concepts.definition: An open-source web application that allows you to create and share documents that contain live code, equations, visualizations and narrative text.

concepts.name: Homebrew
concepts.definition: A free and open-source software package management system that simplifies the installation of software on Apple's macOS operating system.

steps.explanation: Navigate to moderng.xyz to access the course materials.
Sign up to track progress and access quizzes.
Prove completion of exercises to earn the certification.

procedures.name: Installing Python on macOS via Homebrew

steps.explanation: Install the package manager to manage software installations.
Use the package manager to install a specific version of Python and manage versioning.
Look for specific use cases or visual examples of operations.
Find details on specific functions like getting area, bounds, or length of a GeoSeries.

gotchas.context: When installing Python and libraries, users might install packages globally which can cause version conflicts.
gotchas.solution: Use a virtual environment to manage dependencies separately for different projects.

gotchas.context: Users may already have a version of Python installed, which can conflict with the new installation or cause path issues.
gotchas.solution: Be aware of existing installations and use version managers (like Homebrew) to handle different versions.
